title: Comprehensive SQLite Dependency, Import, and Usage Audit

schema.title: SQLite Schema Files (drizzle-orm/sqlite-core + sqliteTable)
schema.summary: 8 SQLite schema files using `sqliteTable` from `drizzle-orm/sqlite-core`. Each has a PG counterpart (`*.pg.sql.ts` using `pgTable` from `drizzle-orm/pg-core`). CRITICAL NUANCE: The `schema.ts` re-export hub (used by all application code) re-exports from PG schemas (`*.pg.sql.ts`), NOT SQLite schemas. SQLite schemas are only used by `drizzle.config.ts` for generating SQLite migrations. The PG schemas are standalone implementations — they do NOT re-export from SQLite schemas.

PG counterpart: control-plane/workspace.pg.sql.ts

ci_cd.title: CI/CD — SQLite References
ci_cd.summary: No SQLite-specific CI jobs. SQLite tests run as part of the standard unit test suite. The CI has dedicated PG and DuckDB jobs but nothing for SQLite.

findings.note: No SQLite-specific job. PG job uses postgres:17 service. DuckDB job uses native duckdb CLI. SQLite tests included in 'test' turbo job.

smoking_guns.title: Smoking Guns — Summary of Critical Findings

items.description: 8 SQLite schema files (`*.sql.ts`) still define all database tables using `sqliteTable` from `drizzle-orm/sqlite-core`. These are used by `drizzle.config.ts` for SQLite migration generation. HOWEVER: `schema.ts` (runtime import hub) re-exports from **PG** schemas (`*.pg.sql.ts`) which are standalone `pgTable` implementations — they do NOT re-export from SQLite schemas. So SQLite schemas are only needed if SQLite migrations are still being generated.
items.runtime_reality: schema.ts (line 1-9) re-exports ALL tables from PG schemas (*.pg.sql.ts). SQLite schemas are only for drizzle.config.ts migration generation.

items.description: migrate.ts still has full SQLite migration support: imports `bun:sqlite`, `drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite`, and `drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite/migrator`. SQLite is the DEFAULT dialect (line 23). The `--sqlite` CLI flag is still supported (line 71).

`makeSQLiteAdapter()` is still active production code and serves as the DEFAULT adapter when Postgres is not configured. The `SQLiteAdapter` Layer is exported. Dual-write adapter uses SQLite as PRIMARY.
drizzle.config.ts still uses `dialect: "sqlite"` and outputs to `./migration` (SQLite migration folder). This config is the default — `db:generate` generates SQLite migrations.
20 SQLite migration folders exist in `packages/opencode/migration/` vs only 4 PG migrations in `migration-pg/`. The PG migration set is far behind.
The entire `packages/effect-drizzle-sqlite/` package (19 source files) is SQLite-exclusive. It depends on `@effect/sql-sqlite-bun` and implements custom Drizzle SQLite drivers for Effect.
3 test files still import `bun:sqlite` and `drizzle-orm/bun-sqlite`. Test fixture `resetDatabase()` used by 30+ files closes/clobbers SQLite database files.

Desktop sidecar.ts has a DEAD `import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/node-sqlite/driver"` — `drizzle` is never called. The entire SQLite migration progress IPC pipeline (7 files) may be dead code as the sidecar no longer runs SQLite migrations.
